problem_statement: The application lacks a centralized interface for managing users. Administrators and owners need to invite users, assign roles, manage enabled status, and remove users without relying on direct database access or scripts.

user_stories.i_want: to add new users by email
user_stories.so_that: I can onboard new team members
I can grant or revoke elevated permissions as organizational needs change

user_stories.i_want: to disable a user's login access
user_stories.so_that: I can temporarily restrict access without deleting the account
I can restore access when appropriate
I can remove users who are no longer part of the organization

user_stories.i_want: to be blocked from accessing user management

must_have: Restrict user management page access to Admin and Owner roles
Display users with email, role, and enabled/disabled status
Allow adding a new user by email with format validation
Default new users to Member role and block duplicate emails
Allow role changes with Owner-only control of Owner role
Allow enabling/disabling users and clearly display disabled status
Allow deleting users with confirmation and prevent self-deletion
should_have: Support search by email and filtering by role/status
Support pagination for large user lists
Prevent self role changes and self-disable actions
Show loading states for asynchronous operations
Display clear error messages for failed operations
nice_to_have: Capture audit logs for admin actions
Allow exporting user list to CSV

acceptance_criteria: Members are denied access to user management
Admins can list users, add users, assign Member/Admin roles, and enable/disable users
Owners can perform all user management actions including Owner role assignment
Invalid email input surfaces a validation error
Duplicate email input surfaces a clear error
Newly added users default to Member role
Disabled users are clearly distinguished in the list
Users cannot delete their own account
Role changes take effect immediately for authorization checks
out_of_scope: Session revocation and active session dashboards
open_questions: Should adding a user trigger an invitation email flow or temporary password flow?
Should the system enforce a minimum Owner count (e.g., cannot demote last Owner)?
Should user deletion be soft-delete or hard-delete?
Should disabling a user immediately invalidate active sessions?
